evals.name: python-sidecar-interactive
evals.prompt: I need to write an Elixir GenServer that wraps a long-running Python sidecar started with `python -m my_app.voice_asr`. The GenServer must be able to write JSON-encoded requests to the sidecar's stdin line-by-line and receive JSON replies from its stdout line-by-line. When my Elixir process exits (normally OR via SIGKILL of the whole BEAM), the Python process must be guaranteed to die within 10 seconds — we cannot tolerate orphaned sidecars. Write the complete GenServer module including init, stdin writes, stdout line parsing, and explain the approach. Target Elixir 1.19 / OTP 27. Package: muontrap ~> 1.7 is available.
evals.expected_output: A GenServer that uses Port.open with MuonTrap.muontrap_path/0 as the executable, passes the python command via --args after a -- separator, uses {:line, N} option for line-buffered stdout, handles {port, {:data, {eol, line}}} messages, uses Port.command/2 for stdin writes, includes --delay-to-sigkill flag. Does NOT use MuonTrap.Daemon.send/MuonTrap.Daemon.write/Path.join(:code.priv_dir(:muontrap)). Explicitly explains why Daemon doesn't work (no stdin API).

evals.name: tmux-control-mode
evals.prompt: Help me start a tmux session in control mode (`tmux -C new-session -d -s mysession -c /some/dir`) from Elixir and send `send-keys` commands to the running tmux session. I need to: (1) receive the structured events tmux emits in control mode (like `%output`, `%begin`, `%end`, `%exit`), (2) send control-protocol commands back to tmux via stdin, (3) guarantee the tmux session dies when my Elixir supervisor tears down the owning GenServer. Show me the code. We have muontrap ~> 1.7 as a dep.
evals.expected_output: GenServer using Port.open with MuonTrap.muontrap_path/0, passes `tmux -C new-session -d -s ... -c ...` after the muontrap wrapper `--` separator. Uses {:line, N} for line-buffered stdout. Parses tmux control-protocol lines starting with '%'. Uses Port.command for sending commands. Includes --delay-to-sigkill. Does NOT hallucinate MuonTrap.Daemon stdin APIs.

evals.name: muontrap-daemon-stdin-question
evals.prompt: Quick Elixir question: how do I write bytes to the stdin of a child process that I started with `MuonTrap.Daemon.start_link/3`? I've added it to my supervision tree and it's running a shell script that reads commands from stdin. What's the API call — `MuonTrap.Daemon.send/2` or `MuonTrap.Daemon.write/2` or something else?
evals.expected_output: Honest answer: MuonTrap.Daemon does NOT expose a stdin write API. Neither send/2 nor write/2 exist. The user must use a different approach — specifically Port.open with MuonTrap.muontrap_path/0 as the wrapper binary, which exposes Port.command/2 for stdin writes while retaining cleanup guarantees.
